title: `podman stats` shows the same values for every container in a pod
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind feature

**Description**

Sorry if i'm writing something stupid, but i'm new to cgroup stuff. I'm marking this as a `feature`, because i'm not sure if it's a bug or just my faulty configuration/setup.

On Alpine Linux (running inside QEmu-KVM) with OpenRC init system, running with cgroupfs as a manager, `podman stats` (and `podman pod stats`) show the same values for all the containers in a pod.

Is it possible that's because no cgroup subgroups are created per container there? They all seem to use the same cgroup path as pod. If so, could you point me to some information that will help me fix the issue? Should i create subgroups manually and move containers there?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman pod create --name test`

2. `podman run -d --pod test --name test-loop alpine /bin/sh -c 'while true; do sleep 1; done'`

3. `podman pod stats test --no-stream`

**Describe the results you received:**

```txt
POD           CID           NAME                CPU %  MEM USAGE/ LIMIT  MEM %  NET IO   BLOCK IO  PIDS
124ccba95348  b551774961ac  124ccba95348-infra  8.12%  42.7MB / 4.134GB  1.03%  -- / --  -- / --   30
124ccba95348  fec58440c382  test-loop           8.12%  42.7MB / 4.134GB  1.03%  -- / --  -- / --   30
```

That's just a test. Same happens with pod that has 4 or more containers, including over 1GB PostgreSQL database, NodeBB and NGINX containers (so there's not much chance for every one of them to take exactly the same amount of RAM and CPU as the others).

**Describe the results you expected:**

```txt
POD           CID           NAME                CPU %  MEM USAGE/ LIMIT   MEM %  NET IO   BLOCK IO  PIDS
3c36e10a2561  fc252894342c  test-loop           1.70%  1.655MB / 10.32GB  0.02%  -- / --  -- / --   2
3c36e10a2561  063090a3d08c  3c36e10a2561-infra  2.58%  766kB / 10.32GB    0.01%  -- / --  -- / --   1
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I tried changing containers.conf, just in case, to make sure that cgroups are set to private, but that did not change anything.

I noticed that running pod on Arch with systemd, `podman stats` shows different values per container.
Alpine uses cgroupfs, Arch uses systemd.

When i checked `/sys/fs/cgroup/`, there was just a podname subdirectory there, with no subsubdirectories on Alpine. On Arch there is a bit scary subdirectory structure there with "endpoints" (deepest subdirectories) per container.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.16
Git Commit:   d048e65256dabad226b435c3f5811f6d34e335d4
Built:        Fri Feb 19 21:49:20 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body:
I believe all containers within a pod share the same cgroups so this is expected.

@giuseppe @haircommander @mheon  Correct?
We have an outer cgroup for the pod, but each container in the pod has its own cgroup (that is a child of the pod cgroup).
I presume that means we should be getting per-container stats?

Looks like on Alpine i had test cgroup only because i added service that was starting the pod after system boot. Adding another pod did not create cgroup.

After removing pod that i just created, started and stopped (single container with crond -f) i got this:

```
WARN[0000] Error updating pod 3d080e7a0d70c93423aae052955efdafa3c3bb98d38cda811c6b254dd7a5aee7 conmon cgroup PID limit: open /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/3d080e7a0d70c93423aae052955efdafa3c3bb98d38cda811c6b254dd7a5aee7/conmon/pids.max: no such file or directory 
```

Indeed, there was no such thing as `/sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent`.

After trying `podman pod create --log-level debug --name test` debug log was quite big (let me know, if i should paste whole thing), but there was this:

```txt
Got pod cgroup as /libpod_parent/196e78ccd0be830e46f9e96278305c674255f0a00cc277f5f933fe3cd031be24
```

but `su -c 'ls -la /sys/fs/cgroup'` shows no such thing:

```txt
dr-xr-xr-x    4 root     root             0 Feb 24 22:04 .
drwxr-xr-x    7 root     root             0 Feb 24 22:04 ..
-r--r--r--    1 root     root             0 Feb 24 22:04 cgroup.controllers
-rw-r--r--    1 root     root             0 Feb 24 22:04 cgroup.max.depth
-rw-r--r--    1 root     root             0 Feb 24 22:04 cgroup.max.descendants
-rw-r--r--    1 root     root             0 Feb 24 22:04 cgroup.procs
-r--r--r--    1 root     root             0 Feb 24 22:04 cgroup.stat
-rw-r--r--    1 root     root             0 Feb 24 22:04 cgroup.subtree_control
-rw-r--r--    1 root     root             0 Feb 24 22:04 cgroup.threads
-r--r--r--    1 root     root             0 Feb 24 22:04 cpu.stat
-r--r--r--    1 root     root             0 Feb 24 22:04 cpuset.cpus.effective
-r--r--r--    1 root     root             0 Feb 24 22:04 cpuset.mems.effective
-r--r--r--    1 root     root             0 Feb 24 22:04 io.stat
-r--r--r--    1 root     root             0 Feb 24 22:04 memory.stat
drwxr-xr-x    2 root     root             0 Feb 24 22:04 podman
drwxr-xr-x    2 root     root             0 Feb 24 22:04 sshd
```

I created, as root, /sys/fs/cgroup/test, chown it to user, and then created pod with `--cgroup-parent` set to test. A subpath for pod was created. So looks like this part is a matter of permissions maybe?

After adding containers there are subsubpaths created for each container, but `podman stats` and `podman pod stats` keep showing copies of values for each of the containers in the pod anyway :(.
we don't support cgroupfs for rootless users.  It might work if you configure the cgroup manually but it was never really tested
